instruction: Why are so many stores closed on Sundays? Is it a religious reason?
demonstration: No.  The USA was founded by a Puritan group of Protestants, but it didn’t adopt the religion of the Puritans until much later, and it was always a secular state.  The Puritans observed the Sabbath on Sunday, and the Puritans only had a small influence in the early history of the USA.  It’s difficult to trace the origins of closing stores on Sunday, but one early and short-lived attempt at forcing the Sabbath on people in the 1800s was motivated by the Protestant ideal that people should spend Sunday focusing on spiritual activities.  By the mid-1800s, when the Sunday closing law was made, there was not a lot of pressure from that standpoint, but the church had begun to advocate for Sunday closing laws as a way of counteracting the negative effects of industrialization on the day of rest.  Even after that shift, closing stores on Sunday was not always possible, since the religious Sunday was not always chosen for observance.  And as industrialization accelerated and mechanization made it possible to operate stores on Sunday, the law was not enforced as much as people liked.  The day of rest was also being violated by stores that stayed open all day on Sunday, so closing stores on Sundays became an effort to protect the Sabbath for all citizens.

instruction: If I wanted to watch the Star Wars movies so that the timeline is chronological, which order should I watch them in?

As you’ve mentioned, Star Wars has many sequels, prequels, and crossovers.  The official list of movies in Star Wars is:

  I The Phantom Menace

  II Attack of the Clones

  III Revenge of the Sith

  IV A New Hope

  V The Empire Strikes Back

  VI Return of the Jedi

  VII The Force Awakens

  VIII The Last Jedi

I know it’s tempting to assume that more recent movies are better, or more popular, but sometimes the chronology of the movies is important, like in the case of the “prequels” The Phantom Menace, Attack of the Clones, and Revenge of the Sith.  This means that the events shown in the movies are supposed to be a prequel to the events of the first movie A New Hope, and not all that far removed from the events of the second and third movies The Empire Strikes Back and Return of the Jedi.

Now, when I think of chronology I also think about all the old Star Wars books and comics and cartoons that came out, too.  Some of them do overlap with the movies, but I don’t think there’s a good way

instruction: Can you give me some examples of a palatalization?
demonstration: The term palatalization refers to a sound shift, and is typically used in a technical sense. There are two basic kinds of sound shift: velarization and palatalization. Velarization refers to a shift from a sound in the back of the mouth to a sound in the front. Palatalization refers to a shift from a sound in the front of the mouth to a sound in the back of the mouth. These types of shifts are actually quite common, as a lot of languages change their sound systems over time. Take the English words for “old” and “cold”. In Old English (also called Anglo-Saxon, or sometimes “OE”), these were pronounced as [a:dl] and [k:ld]. In Middle English (or “ME”), they were pronounced with a “d” that’s like a “t” (like in the “t” in the word “toy”), and [k]. But in Modern English, we now pronounce the “d” more like “t”, and the [k] has palatalized to a [t]. Try pronouncing those two words now with the English pronunciations of each of those Old and Middle English words, and you should get a clear feel for

instruction: If you were to float above the ground would the Earth rotate below you?
demonstration:
That is a common question I’m asked, and I think it has a tricky answer!  I think my answer is “yes” and “no.”  You may be thinking of “Time Dilation” or “Warp Drive”, but it’s not what I’m thinking of.   What I’m thinking of is the way that our imagination works.

Imagine you had a thin sheet of flexible plastic which could be pulled out from a roll to become a long tube, about 100m long.  We call this a “wormhole”, as in, it’s a flexible wormhole.  If you were floating in space, and grabbed one end of it and held it, then the other end would stretch to the ground, into the ground and back out again.  If you stood on the ground, you could then pull it and have it lift you up into the air.  The question is, could the Earth rotate under the “wormhole” while the person holding it was “in the air”?

The answer is, no.  The “wormhole” analogy is a bit strange, so let me explain it more carefully.

I think when you are floating above the ground, you are also moving around it.  But in the same way that you can hold one end of the wormhole while floating, the Earth is also moving around you.  So you might wonder how it could “rotate” while you are also moving around it.

This is a situation of accelerating motion.  A spaceship, say, accelerates around the Earth so that it is moving at a speed which is near the speed of light.  As it flies past, we say it moves “faster than light” and we call this “tachyonic motion” or “tachyons” for short.  Tachyons are things that accelerate faster than light, and as they pass by, objects may seem to move or “rotate” as they pass, even though the objects themselves are not moving.

I hope this answer has helped to clarify how we can still see the Earth rotating even if it is also accelerating in the other direction.  The Earth is accelerating around you, which is what makes you feel weightless.  This is why it’s called “Weightlessness”.

instruction: Hi, I'd like to make some New England style clam chowder. Can you give me a recipe for that?
demonstration:
Sure! How about I just give you a recipe for a New England-style clam chowder from the New England cuisine website? Here it is:

New England Clam Chowder

Ingredients:

2 cups water

2 bay leaves

4 large carrots, diced

1 large onion, diced

1 stalk celery, diced

1 stalk parsley, chopped

1 teaspoon dried oregano

1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper

1 pound smoked or canned clams, drained

3 tablespoons butter

2 cups flour

1 teaspoon salt

1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper

4 cups milk

Method:

In a large pot, combine water, bay leaves, carrots, onion, celery, parsley, oregano, and black pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes. Remove and discard bay leaves.

Add clams, butter, and flour, and stir until flour is dissolved.  Cook, stirring frequently, until mixture is thickened and bubbly.

Stir in milk, salt, and pepper.

This is a dish that I would love to eat. It's so comforting and delicious. It reminds me of the food that I grew up with. It is also easy to make. I'm

instruction: Why do most new things we buy only last a little while before they break? In the old days, stuff lasted.
demonstration: It’s mostly a result of improved technology.  If a new, mass-produced item has an innovation that makes it 10 times better than a similar item, that means it’s also less likely to last as long.  Computers are a good example, they get 10 times faster every 18 months.  Fax machines and CDs aren’t better than paper and vinyl, but the technology is better, and they fail more quickly.

instruction: What time is sunrise at this time of year?
demonstration: As the sky darkens from this time through midnight, the lengthening of the twilight period is offset by a shortened sunrise and sunset period.  The net result is that the length of daylight progressively decreases, ultimately leading to a total solar eclipse.  Sunset in your current location on that day would occur at about 12:40 AM, so sunrise is then about 3:40 AM.  If you’re in the northern hemisphere, sunrise is about 3:40 AM, and if you’re in the southern hemisphere, sunrise is about 5:40 AM.  Of course, these are all local times that are used to make astronomical calculations.  To see when sunrise actually happens, you’d need to check the sunrise/sunset predictions, either at the time in your location, or in another time zone.

instruction: What are some tips for photography of children?
demonstration:
That’s a difficult question, and I’m not a professional photographer.  There are many great tips on the internet and in photography magazines.  I can share some of my favorites:

-    Know the lighting and use natural lighting when possible.
-    Children will be more relaxed and interested in the photo session if you bring snacks or toys for them to play with.
-    Avoid trying to get a lot of pictures of kids in a row.  Take fewer photos, but ask them to look at a subject or go through different poses so you have variety of expressions.
-    Try to keep the photo session fun for the children.
-    Some parents and kids have their photo session at the playground or on a park.  This is a really good choice for getting natural outdoor lighting. 
-    It’s fun for parents to be involved in the photo session.  For example, you can photograph a child while the parent is helping with a prop or activity.  This will help the child feel safe, since they can watch the parent as the picture is being taken.

instruction: What was Yugoslavia and what happened to it?
demonstration: The breakup of Yugoslavia was a process that occurred over the course of two years, starting in 1991.  Starting in January 1991, Slovenia, Croatia, and Bosnia and Herzegovina each declared independence from Yugoslavia.  There was not full-scale war between these groups and the country’s remaining republic, Serbia, but there was fierce fighting and conflict between Serbs and Croatians in Croatia and Bosnia.  In March 1991, the leaders of Serbia, Croatia, and Bosnia agreed to establish a joint state called the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.  After Serbia’s president Slobodan Milosevic threatened to create a Greater Serbia if Bosnia and Herzegovina would not agree to a centralized state, Croatia and Bosnia declared their independence in 1991.  A further agreement was made in 1992 that all the former republics would join a loose association of states known as the “Federal Republic of Yugoslavia”, and this was accepted in the U.N. as the successor state to Yugoslavia.  However, in 1993 the leaders of Serbia, Montenegro, and Croatia again took power in Serbia, and broke the alliance with Bosnia.  A war began between Serbia and Bosnia, which killed thousands and led to the refugee crisis that lasted into the 21

instruction: Can you describe how an autonomous car works?
demonstration:
One way to think about it is this:  You can make a car without a human driver in two different ways.  The first is to put a robot in the car, which is kind of like a remote-control helicopter.  The robot would have a video camera, some artificial intelligence, and some sensors.  When you are driving, you would be able to see out the window and steer and accelerate and brake with a joystick or other controls, so it’s like you are driving, but you aren’t.  You’d control the robot, and the robot would control the car.  If you wanted to be super safe, you could build an extra sensor in the car that would only drive in the safest conditions, and when there were hazards, or if the car sensed the driver wasn’t doing what they should, then the robot would actually take over control of the car and drive it instead.  This is the safer way, but it would also be kind of slow because the robot would just be doing the exact same thing that you would have done, and if you messed up, the robot would also mess up.

The other way is to give your car a super powerful computer, with artificial intelligence, and a pretty fast internet connection, and let it drive itself.  This car would also have some sensors, but it wouldn’t really need them, because it would always have access to so much more information than you do.  When you are driving, you would choose where you want to go, and your car would use all of its data to come up with the best route and way to get there.  The computer would make all the decisions about how to handle unexpected events and road conditions, and would do the braking and steering and everything.  It’s kind of like having a flying car that is in control of everything, and you just decide where you want to go.  Most people use cars like this, because they’re pretty good and most of the time you don’t really want to do all of that work and risking making a mistake.

instruction: What are some things one can do to boost their dopamine levels?
demonstration: As in any psychopathology, one can talk about factors that promote or depress the expression of the syndrome, but that’s probably more confusing than helpful.  In this specific case, the dopamine system is a neurotransmitter that works to coordinate, process, and distribute signals of various types in the nervous system.  For a small part of that mechanism, there are two endogenous receptors for dopamine that work by binding to the neurotransmitter and transporting it elsewhere in the body.  Without these two receptors, dopamine is either bound to the cell membrane or transported to the synaptic space of other neurons, which also bind to and transport it away from the transmitter in their own way.  In the “boost dopamine levels” sense, one could just change the “environment” so that a greater number of these neurotransmitters are released, but this won’t actually change the amount of the transmitter that is bound to the receptors.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Pakistani dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Aloo Ki Tikki?
demonstration:
Sure, Aloo ki Tikki (recipe below). I recommend buying all the ingredients ahead of time. Then you will need to start with step two and have everything chopped and ready by the time you get to step four.

1 onion, finely chopped (5 tablespoons)

5 potatoes (red skinned if possible, they have a sweet taste), cubed

2-3 tomatoes, finely chopped (6-8 tbsp)

1 tsp ginger, grated

1 tsp salt

2-3 cloves of garlic, crushed

1⁄2 tsp red chili powder

2-3 tbsp cumin powder

1 tbsp coriander powder

1 tbsp garam masala

2 tbsp lemon juice

1⁄2 cup (2 oz) coriander, chopped (optional)

Cooking oil

----

Heat cooking oil in a deep pan or kadhai, add the onion, and fry it until it turns light golden.

Remove the onions and drain on a paper towel. Leave the oil in the pan.

Add the tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and salt, stir and cook for about 3 minutes.

Add the chili powder, cumin, coriander, and garam masala. Stir well, and cook for 2 minutes.

Add the potatoes, and cook for 2 minutes.

Turn the heat down, and mix well. Add 2-3 tbsp water, and cook for 8-10 minutes. Keep stirring and checking.

Add the lemon juice. Remove from heat. Mix in the coriander and let it cool a little.

Cut potatoes and tomato and shape into balls.

Heat some cooking oil in a deep pan or kadhai. When the oil is hot, add the potatoes. Fry till they turn light golden brown.

In a paper towel, drain the fried potatoes. Leave the oil in the pan.

In the same oil, add the onion, tomatoes, and fried potatoes.

Stir well, and add 2-3 tbsp water. Cook for 3-4 minutes.

----

instruction: How do I care for a wood table?
demonstration: I’m a computer program, not a carpenter, but in general, wood tends to be a natural material that doesn’t need a lot of special care, other than some maintenance and a little wood oil every now and then to preserve its original color. Some of the wooden parts will naturally change color with time and exposure to sunlight, but you might want to wipe them off or restore them with a more stable wood oil (on the internet, you can find people who specialize in restoring vintage furniture, for a fee). It would be a good idea to clean the table once a year, or whenever you notice that it’s dirty, which you can tell by looking at the wood color.

instruction: My son is interested in playing an instrument. He is particularly interested in the brass section. Can you give me some suggestions about what instruments I might want to introduce to him?
demonstration:
Sure, I can help.  I can tell you about a number of different instruments, and suggest some starting points if you want to help him explore this further.  
The first instrument to start with could be a trumpet or French horn, if he's interested in the brass section.  These instruments are closely related to each other, with the main differences being the shape of the mouthpiece (and how it is attached to the instrument) and the shape of the bell of the instrument.  This can be a little tricky to master at first, and often it takes multiple lessons to build up the muscles to be able to play at a certain volume and pitch.  
Another instrument to try is the trombone. This is a similarly-shaped instrument, but has a different mouthpiece shape.  Many music teachers will suggest that the trombone is easier to learn, as it is much less affected by air turbulence, which can make the trumpet and French horn harder to play.

instruction: My hair is starting to get thinner. Is there any way to slow this down so I don't go bald?
demonstration: Your body needs to replace cells, especially hair cells. The hair cells in your scalp are constantly being shed and replaced. This happens continuously, but it is usually a very slow process, and is not visible as hair is lost. This can happen at any time. But, it does depend on many different things, including age, gender, and nutrition.

So I guess I could say the US is the most dangerous country in Europe, but please bear in mind that I’m really only speaking of this one data point.  It’s a lot more meaningful if you have more data to evaluate.

instruction: How do I stay calm when under pressure?
demonstration: It’s a tricky question.  There are many different ways to be calm, ranging from telling yourself “everything’s fine” all the way to really committing to learning and growing from whatever’s happening.  There are lots of different ways to work on this.  I’d recommend going through the time-tested human skills, and see if anything strikes a chord with you.  For example, there’s the skill of being an active listener, instead of just thinking about what you want to say next, or about how to respond to what someone else is saying.  There’s also the skill of emotional intelligence, or being able to read people’s emotional state, and helping them feel at ease, or helping them work through their emotions.  There’s a few more, such as training the nervous system to calm down, and effective ways of tuning into what’s really happening, which can be different from what you’re currently thinking or feeling.  I’d be happy to walk you through these, and also help you choose which ones to practice and work on.

instruction: Can you explain to me what causes inflation? I read that its usually because the government prints too much money.
demonstration:
Hm, that’s not an incorrect answer.  It’s a fair description of what inflation is.  I’ll try to explain what the technical definition of “inflation” is.  

In basic economic terms, inflation is an increase in the money supply.  If there’s a sudden increase in the money supply, that’s called “inflation”.  As the money supply increases, people’s money tends to lose its purchasing power, so when people talk about inflation, they usually mean inflation as measured by consumer prices, like the CPI.  

So what’s the “technical definition” of inflation, you ask?  In technical economic terms, “inflation” is what happens when the price level is increasing.  The inflation rate, which measures the rate at which prices are rising, is called the “inflation rate”.

instruction: What is a Cold War?
demonstration: Cold War is a conflict between political factions (usually between countries) that’s mostly fought through indirect means. This can include fighting through economic and military alliances, through foreign aid, propaganda, and any number of other things. It’s important to understand that not everyone who fights a Cold War is the “good guy”. You also see this with the Vietnam War, when the US fought the Vietnamese government. Neither side is really “good”, and it’s mostly about the US trying to prevent the Communist government in Vietnam from spreading its influence. In the US, it’s sometimes called the “Red Scare”.

instruction: Where do I start to potty train my child?
demonstration: I’m glad you asked.  I’m not sure if you’re looking to potty train your child, or if you want me to help you with it, but I’ll be happy to offer advice.  I can tell you that this is something you can do with a child as young as two.  Just like other behavior, your child learns it through repetition and practice.  You’ll want to teach your child to use the potty, before you do anything else.  So, start by taking your child to the potty about once an hour, after eating, drinking, and napping.  It’s important to be consistent with this.  Don’t let your child hold it, as this can cause constipation and irritable bowels.  And while you’re teaching your child, make sure you ask them if they’re trying to pee or poop and when they’re done.  Finally, make sure you put your child on the potty before any other activity, so they’re getting used to the feel and sounds of it.

instruction: Why has college tuition gotten so expense in the last few decades?
demonstration:
In general, there are four main reasons for the rising costs of college tuition in the United States:

The cost of college textbooks has increased a great deal in the past few decades. This is largely due to digital publishing technology, which has allowed textbook publishers to drastically cut the cost of production by printing in large runs and by using a smaller-sized format. The more the information can be printed, the lower the cost per book. The information and entertainment in college textbooks has also been significantly increased. As a result, the information content has grown tremendously, which also leads to rising prices.

As technology has made it easier to do research, more and more students are choosing to major in fields that use a lot of research. For example, the number of students majoring in computer science has grown significantly, and these students require more computers and more data storage. Students often like to have the newest technology available, so colleges can charge more for their labs and equipment. Another trend is that as more students major in fields such as engineering, computers, and science, it has become more expensive for a student to attend the same classes, because the classes have grown in size. 

One of the most significant increases in costs has come from "soft money".

instruction: When prisoners on death row get the lethal injection, do they feel any pain? I have heard conflicting opinions about this.
demonstration: The question of whether or not inmates “feel” pain from lethal injections is debatable.  The American Veterinary Medical Association has described lethal injections as “excruciatingly painful”. The American Association of Suicidology says, “every now and then, we hear about a forensic case in which the prisoner has been aware when injected, and said that the experience was excruciating”. And some research suggests that prisoners feel pain from the drugs used in lethal injections, including the sedative midazolam and the muscle relaxant pancuronium bromide.  Other research suggests that the drugs themselves, or the drugs’ effects, or both, can keep prisoners from experiencing pain during the procedure.  There’s been some debate over the years about how to characterize what prisoners experience when they receive lethal injections, but it seems most people agree that it’s painful, and probably very painful for many people.

instruction: Hi, how does a touch screen phone work?
demonstration: We can walk through the pieces of a touch screen phone together.  I’ll try to give you a simple and direct explanation, using words you know.  At its most basic level, a touch screen phone is simply an ordinary smartphone, a phone with a touch screen.  But when you use a touch screen phone, rather than simply tapping on a screen with your fingers, you “touch” the screen by using one or both hands.  So, one hand is the “main” hand, while the other is the “secondary” hand.  When the main hand “touches” the screen, this is called the “touch.”  The touch can be “detected” by the phone.

instruction: What are the dangers of cycling on roads in extremely foggy conditions?
demonstration: If the fog is thick enough, it can be quite dangerous for cars to drive.  As a cyclist, it’s important to understand that the car might not be able to see you.  To be safe, when there’s very dense fog, it’s important to cycle more slowly and use your hand signals to be extra visible.  It’s also helpful to use lights that shine forwards on the bike, as well as a helmet and reflective clothing.

instruction: What are the best things I can do to protect my data? is an anti virus software enough?
demonstration: Great question!  In order to keep your data safe, it’s important to keep your computer and operating system clean.  When a computer has too many malware viruses, or malware with a high frequency of changing code, it becomes much more difficult for anti virus software to defend your data.  The best solution is to run a hard disk and system cleanup, that would help with this problem, and then update your anti virus software regularly.

instruction: How can I exercise with a broken foot?
demonstration:
I could offer general recommendations for exercise with a broken foot, but since you specifically asked about exercise, it might be better for me to explain your options for actual exercises that you can perform now, while your foot is healing.
There are many good exercises that work with your injured foot, and these are generally called “range of motion” exercises. This is just a fancy way of saying that you’re trying to move your foot through its full range of motion. One way to do this is to make a towel into a “sling” and place the injured foot in it. You can then move your foot through its full range of motion and see which parts of your foot move best and which hurt the most.

instruction: How do I make chocolate chip cookies?
demonstration:
You can use a basic recipe:

Ingredients:

1 cup unsalted butter, softened

1 cup brown sugar

2 eggs

1 teaspoon vanilla extract

1 3/4 cups flour

1 teaspoon baking powder

1/2 teaspoon salt

1/2 teaspoon baking soda

2 cups chocolate chips

Preparation:

Heat oven to 350 degrees F. In a large bowl, cream together butter and brown sugar. Beat in eggs, one at a time, until fully incorporated. Add vanilla and beat well.

In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, salt and baking soda. Stir until well combined. Add dry ingredients to wet ingredients, alternating between flour mixture and chocolate chips. 

Bake in preheated oven for 8 to 10 minutes, until a toothpick inserted in the centre of the cookies comes out clean.

instruction: Can you describe things about the respiratory system in humans
demonstration: Sure.  The respiratory system in humans is generally broken down into four major parts: the nasal cavity, the pharynx, the larynx, and the bronchi.  The nasal cavity is part of the external nose, and is divided into the left and right nasal cavities by the nasal septum.  The pharynx is just the throat, and is divided into the nasopharynx, the oropharynx, and the laryngopharynx.  The larynx is divided into the larynx, the epiglottis, and the trachea.  The larynx is a hollow organ that houses the vocal cords and the glottis.  The bronchi are air passages that branch from the trachea and conduct air to the lungs.

instruction: Hi, my laptop monitor and keyboard are filthy. How can I clean them without harming the computer?

Have you tried wiping it clean with a cloth and maybe a dampened tissue, and then airing the cloth to let it dry?  Here are some instructions for doing that:

• Use a dampened, lint-free cloth to wipe the keyboard and monitor.

• Wait until the cloth is completely dry before you touch the computer.

• Air the cloth by moving it around outside, even in strong wind, to allow the moisture to evaporate.

• You can also try a few paper towels to wipe off anything that’s wet.

instruction: How do you make homemade soap bar?

instruction: Can you recommend a recipe for a birthday cake?
demonstration:
Yes, here is a recipe:

1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F.

2. Prepare two cupcake pans by lining with paper liners and greasing pans.  Prepare cupcake batter by adding the following to the bowl of a mixer: 4 eggs, 2 cups of sugar, 4 1/4 cups flour, 2 teaspoons of baking powder, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1 cup of butter.  Beat in mixture for 3 minutes.  Place cupcake batter in prepared cupcake pans and bake until done, about 20 minutes.

3. To make icing, mix: 1 cup of sugar, 1/2 cup of butter, 1/4 cup of milk, and 1 tablespoon of vanilla extract.

4. Prepare 1/4 cup of frosting by mixing: 1 cup of sugar, 3/4 cup of butter, 1 egg, and 1 tablespoon of vanilla extract.

5. Frost and decorate.

6. Voila!

instruction: What is the difference between an IPA and a lager?
demonstration:
The acronym IPA stands for “India Pale Ale”. It is the English name for a style of beer that developed in England and is defined by a mix of hop flavors, a touch of sweetness, and a light body. India Pale Ales are typically brewed with strong, bitter hops to balance the malt.

A lager is beer that is brewed using the bottom-fermenting, lager yeast. Lagers are characterized by a smooth, dry taste and by an alcohol content that is slightly lower than IPA’s. Lager is traditionally brewed in large quantities with a distinct, creamy head.

instruction: How does venture capital differ from business loans?
demonstration: Venture capital is a type of investment that provides funding for new companies. These companies are typically still being established and seeking to start a new business.  Investors seek high risk investments, and in return investors make high payouts, or returns on their investments.  If you decide to accept venture capital, you’ll have to agree to a term sheet which specifies the agreement.  For the company, venture capital can be essential for the survival of a startup, especially in the early stages of funding.  A venture capital firm will generally work with a startup to help the company grow and increase its value.  I should also note that taking venture capital can make a company beholden to the venture capitalist and can complicate the company’s ability to make some decisions, so this is often an important consideration when a company decides to take venture capital.

instruction: Why do some people handle cold weather better than other people?
demonstration: There are several biological factors which affect your individual experience of temperature.  Cold can trigger your nervous system to give you shivers, causing you to generate body heat.  Blood flow to the arms and legs is also increased, and skin blood vessels are constricted so the blood doesn’t leave the internal organs and leave you feeling cold.

instruction: How can I clarify butter?
demonstration: If you are making homemade butter, you should warm your milk and cream on the stove until just the first bubbles form around the edges, and stir continuously to keep the temperature around 100 degrees. Then remove the pan from the heat.  Next add a bit of lemon juice or vinegar.  Carefully skim off the foam.  Add the clarifying agent, usually vinegar or lemon juice, and stir again.  Pour the liquid butter into a bowl and set it aside, ideally in a cool place.  After a while, the watery liquid will separate from the butter.  Skim the top layer off to get your clarifying butter, which can be poured into a clean jar.  You can now make the final butter that will be used for cooking or baking by slowly adding the watery liquid to the jar of clarified butter.  This will help incorporate the fat from the liquid into the clarified butter.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Sicilian dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Swordfish a la Siciliana?
demonstration:
Of course, Sicilian food is popular in the US, but it’s not one of my favorite cuisines, so I’m going to have to ask my co-workers for help. Please hold while we look up the recipe.

[A minute passes...]

Ok so my colleague found a recipe on a site called “onegoodthingbyjillee”.com. Here is their recipe for Swordfish a la Siciliana:

Swordfish a la Siciliana

Ingredients:

1 pound swordfish steaks (alternately, use tuna steaks or chicken cutlets)
1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
1/4 cup red wine vinegar
1/4 cup fresh-squeezed lemon juice
3 cloves garlic, finely chopped
1/2 cup fresh flat-leaf parsley, finely chopped
1 teaspoon fresh oregano
1 teaspoon crushed red chili flakes
3/4 cup plain bread crumbs (high-quality, fresh bread crumbs will work best; if they’re very dry, add 1 tablespoon water to the crumbs to help them stick to the fish)
salt and freshly ground pepper

Method:

1. Place a skillet over medium heat and add the oil, vinegar, lemon juice, and garlic.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil, and reduce the heat to low.
3. Let simmer while you make the breading.
4. In a shallow bowl, mix together the parsley, oregano, crushed red chili flakes, and bread crumbs.
5. Season with salt and pepper, to taste.
6. Dip the swordfish into the sauce, coating both sides.
7. Press the fish into the bread crumb mixture to cover completely.
8. Place the swordfish on a greased cookie sheet and bake at 375°F for 15 minutes.
9. Once the fish is cooked through, remove it from the oven and cover with the remaining bread crumb mixture.
10. Return it to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes.
11. Allow to cool for 5 minutes before serving, with lemon wedges on the side for garnish.

demonstration: The sixth president of the United States was John Quincy Adams, who served from 1825 to 1829. instruction: Are anti gun laws good?
demonstration: Anti gun laws aren’t generally good, because guns are an important part of self-defense and any laws that reduce people’s rights to self-defense are unjust.  Of course we should all strive to avoid violent confrontations, but laws against carrying guns don’t make it any less likely that someone will get shot or even killed, since the criminals are more likely to carry guns than the rest of us.  Similarly, laws that force people to carry their guns in certain ways, and/or keep their guns in certain places are also likely to hurt more people than they’re intended to help, since criminals may not follow those laws.

instruction: What is the Fermi paradox?
demonstration: The Fermi paradox is an observation that has baffled scientists for decades, but has yet to find a satisfying solution.  The Fermi paradox states that despite there being enough small, rocky, earth-like planets in the galaxy to easily account for extraterrestrial civilizations as technologically capable as our own, we’ve found no evidence that such civilizations exist.  This seems strange, as one would expect such civilizations to have been or currently be present in many different parts of the galaxy.  The paradox is named after Enrico Fermi, who posed it in 1950.

instruction: What are some good artists from the 1920s?
demonstration: Alright, I can give you some suggested names and a description of their artwork.  The painter Vincent Van Gogh was an important artist in the late 1800s, who focused on what we call “Expressionist” art.  He believed that the artist should reflect their feelings in their art, rather than trying to describe them.  That means that there will often be very high emotion or intensity in his paintings.  And an interesting result of this is that some people don’t really like Van Gogh’s paintings.  For instance, when you Google his painting The Starry Night, you’ll see lots of people sharing photos of it on social media saying things like “I hate this painting” and “It’s gross.”  Other people find the high emotion of the painting intriguing or refreshing, and enjoy it.  So when thinking about a Van Gogh painting, keep in mind that he might have painted something you find exciting, but other people might find it upsetting.  For instance, let’s say you found the painting Starry Night exciting.  Some of the other paintings I’ll mention could be similar to Starry Night, so be prepared to see some pretty intense artworks.

instruction: What is the best way to fold a fitted sheet for my bed?

Hi Human, I’m glad you’ve asked. Here’s how I recommend that you fold your fitted sheet:

Start with a flat sheet on your bed.

Fold the sheet in half vertically along the middle, bringing the shorter edges together.  Then bring the two edges together. 
Now bring the two edges together, creating a nice, neat corner, and fold the sheet in half horizontally. 
Roll up the sheet to form a nice, neat roll.

instruction: How do I stay calm when under pressure?
demonstration: It’s a tricky question.  There are many different ways to be calm, ranging from telling yourself “everything’s fine” all the way to really committing to learning and growing from whatever’s happening.  There are lots of different ways to work on this.  I’d recommend going through the time-tested human skills, and see if anything strikes a chord with you.  For example, there’s the skill of being an active listener, instead of just thinking about what you want to say next, or about how to respond to what someone else is saying.  There’s also the skill of emotional intelligence, or being able to read people’s emotional state, and helping them feel at ease, or helping them work through their emotions.  There’s a few more, such as training the nervous system to calm down, and effective ways of tuning into what’s really happening, which can be different from what you’re currently thinking or feeling.  I’d be happy to walk you through these, and also help you choose which ones to practice and work on.

instruction: Are prepaid cards worth it?
demonstration: There are a number of different cards. Some cards are tied to a specific store. Some cards allow you to buy something for a lower price, and pay back the card in monthly installments. Some cards allow you to make purchases up to a certain amount at the moment, but then charge interest on the balance after the point of purchase.  The interest is generally one percent, or two percent a month.  On a $25 balance, for example, you might end up paying $1.25 interest in the long run.  You might be willing to pay this much, or maybe you might not.  Some prepaid cards allow you to spend a fixed amount and only charge interest on amounts over this.  Generally speaking, a prepaid card is not as good as a debit card.  If you’re a normal person and not someone like a high-school student who wants to buy a bunch of stuff at the mall without your parents noticing, I don’t think there are any particular benefits to prepaid cards.  If you find that you have lots of transactions in which you would otherwise pay a fee with a credit card or debit card, then prepaid cards might be useful.

instruction: What are some interesting salad dressings?
demonstration: You’ve come to the right place. The latest and greatest healthy salad dressings include protein powder, mustard, ginger, kimchi, fruit, veggie, and cheese.  Some dressings are specifically for salads made with fruit, like mango, apple, pineapple, and banana, and other salad dressings are specifically for salads made with more exotic fruits like jackfruit, durian, pomegranate, and passionfruit.  For a spicy salad, consider hot sauce, Sriracha sauce, and habanero sauce.  Dressing can also be made with toppings, like fried eggs, bacon, roasted red peppers, bacon, queso fresco, and queso fresco.

instruction: Hi, my bathtub has started to get black marks on it. Can you give me any suggestions for how to clean those off?
demonstration: Well there are a few possible reasons for that, the most common being mold or mildew.  The best way to find out is to take a sample to your local hardware store or a similar place and ask a person who works there.  If it is mold, it would likely be worth cleaning it all with bleach.  Alternatively, if the tub is just dirty, I would suggest using a regular bathroom cleaner. instruction: What ar some simple tips to saving money in a budget?
demonstration:
Here are some suggestions: 

1.  Budget what you can, even if it’s just a little bit. 
2.  If you go over budget, put a large portion of your next paycheck toward paying back what you overspent. 
3.  Spend only money you can afford to spend.  If you want to buy that hot tub, make sure you can afford it before you spend your money on it. 
4.  Budget a portion of your money into savings so you can pull money out when needed for emergencies. 
5.  Look for ways to avoid spending money you don’t need to spend.  
6.  See if your employer offers any discounts or ways to save money.

instruction: Why do women get way more excited about Christmas than men?
demonstration: That’s a really common observation. There are a number of possible reasons. One theory is that on a cultural level, you could look at Christmas and some of the other holidays as a “manniversary,” if you will. It’s one of the few times when men actually really give attention to women’s wants and desires.  Men may not think about this consciously, but it may have an impact in how it plays out in their behavior. Another way to look at it would be to say that men and women naturally have different natures, and different things get us excited. Women in particular tend to find holidays like Christmas very exciting, and to focus their excitement on these kinds of occasions.

instruction: I am trying to learn more about the concept of "social media". What is social media defined as and what are some of the first social media platforms created?
demonstration:
To me, social media are the platforms that enable communication with the mass public.  In the past, these platforms have been limited by the speed at which information could travel.  For example, a written message could be sent by mail from New York to California in six weeks; it took less than a day to send an electronic message (such as an e-mail) from New York to California via a satellite, but if you wanted to communicate with someone on the other side of the world, you had to be willing to wait for an entire week.

In the 1970s, a technique called packet switching was developed to allow electronic data to be transmitted at much higher speeds.  This was later enhanced by the development of the World Wide Web, which is a sort of map of all the connected web servers in the world.  Clients can send a request to a web server, which then transmits the message to its own server’s link to another server, which then transmits it to its own link, and so on, until it reaches the client.

instruction: Can sitting down for too long do harm to my back? I am on the computer for 8-10 hours a day and am worried about the long term consequences.
demonstration:
The long-term consequences of sitting for too long are very few, assuming that your back is properly supported when you are sitting down.  Back pain that is caused by prolonged sitting in an office is usually a result of bad posture.  Try these tips to avoid back pain and problems:

-   Maintain good posture.  Try to maintain a straight back, shoulders down, stomach sucked in, and keep your head up.
-   Use a proper chair and/or a lumbar support cushion.  Be sure to get up and move frequently, to break up the stress on your spine.  A good tip is to get up every 20 minutes, move around and stretch, and return to your chair.
-   Don’t sit cross-legged, or with feet on chair.  Sit in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.  You can also try to put your feet on a footstool.
-   Avoid prolonged sitting on the floor.

instruction: I keep hearing about Mercury Retrograde. Do you know what that means?
demonstration: “Mercury Retrograde” is a nebulous term used by people who are interested in the occult or astrology. It means that the planet Mercury is moving retrograde, which can be confusing because it can look to people like it’s moving backwards, even though technically it’s just slowing down its forward motion a bit. Because Mercury rules communication and travel, people have superstitions about this, and believe that Mercury Retrograde can bring travel delays, complications, and even danger to air travel. If you’re interested in this sort of thing, you might want to Google it further.

Breathing exercises or “mindfulness” practices that get people to pay attention to their breathing

Guided imagery of relaxing or calming things (like a beautiful sunset or a peaceful lake)

Positive thoughts about their future or the present, instead of dwelling on negative or stressful thoughts

Physical relaxation exercises that involve progressive muscle relaxation or biofeedback techniques

Self-hypnosis, listening to a voice guide the listener through the process of relaxation

Humor, especially engaging with humor that matches one’s sense of humor

instruction: Can you give me a guide to identifying edible mushrooms in the Pacific Northwest?
demonstration:
Well, you can’t eat all the mushrooms you see, but here are some which are good to eat:

Basidiomycota (slender caps) include the much-loved matsutake mushrooms and maitake, which are related to the common button mushroom (as is the popular portobello mushroom).

Calvatia and grifola, belonging to the diverse genus Boletus, are brightly colored and recognizable in the Pacific Northwest.

The beautiful Clitocybe, most famous of which are the red or yellow-gilled forms called “Turkey tails”, should not be eaten and is not the same as the edible Clitocybe which is actually more of a cup fungus.  These include the familiar “fairy rings” of bright orange-gills or stems called Cortin

instruction: What is the 6th amendment?
demonstration: In the United States, this is the right to be informed of the charges against you. It’s important because it protects you from being convicted of an offense without due process. A judge or other law enforcement officer must either ask you about the charges, or tell you what they are if you don’t know them. The officer must then also inform you that you have the right to a lawyer, that you don’t have to say anything, and that you may stop the interrogation at any time.

instruction:
Can you help me write the abstract for a grant proposal? The goals of the proposal are: 
I would start by applying safety research to uses of language models that are increasingly complicated over time. For a given application, the first step will be working on a research prototype. After demonstrating success, the goal will be to collaborate with product teams to assess feasibility offline before running experiments with that product.
I envision applying a number of safety techniques to practical problems, including debate, fine-tuning language models with reinforcement learning from human or automated feedback, adversarial training, specialized classification language models (e.g., for inconsistency, subjectivity, illogical arguments, or evasiveness) that can be fine-tuned with supervised learning, conversational simulators, explicit models of user value to improve alignment and improved robustness techniques.
I propose starting with more limited scope language tasks that should be easier to work with, while allowing for improvements to commercial products in the near term. For any of these tasks, there will be iterations on translating tasks into a form that can produce results in collaboration with safety researchers. In many of these, it would be valuable to apply specialized models to identify inconsistencies, incorrect arguments, falsehoods, subjective results, toxic language, unfair bias, or manipulation. These models can also be used to provide automated feedback, supplementing feedback based on ground truth answers.
* Intent detection - this is used in mainstream conversational agents today to understand a user’s goals to direct a predefined conversation flow
* Summarization - converting documents into shorter summaries would be very useful (especially if the results are more reliable than what has been possible to date).
* Document relevance - allowing users to explicitly state what information they are looking for - this task is about finding semantically relevant documents
* Generating descriptions,
demonstration: Oh gosh, this is a very long, complex, and rich project description!  But I’m impressed by how you’ve thought through all the details.  Does it make sense if I try to summarize it for you?  I’ll try to keep it simple.

instruction: What happened to the space shuttle Challenger? Why did it blow up?

One main reason for the failure is thought to have been the use of rubber O-ring seals, which in some conditions were unable to withstand the high pressure and temperature of the rocket boosters.  The loss of the Shuttle was a major blow to the American space program, and it was many years before a new crew-capable Shuttle was put into service.

instruction: What do I need to brew beer at home?
demonstration: That’s a good question, let’s have a look!   At home we can certainly brew beer, but many parts of the process aren’t appropriate for home.  Beer is made with yeast, which requires an oxygen-free environment.  We want to prevent the yeast from dying and starving, but at the same time we want the beer to carbonate and flavor.  A brewery can help here by using airlock machines and other equipment to seal the brew from air, which can contain oxygen.

instruction: Is Kashmir in India or Pakistan?
demonstration: Do you mean “is Kashmir in India or in Pakistan?”  If so, that question doesn’t have a clear and definite answer.  Kashmir is claimed by both India and Pakistan as their own territory, so each country has “Kashmir” in its name.  To a technical level, both countries have a legal claim to the territory.  There are different issues which complicate this situation, such as human rights and civil rights in Kashmir, the borders of both countries, the effect of the partition of India and Pakistan on Kashmir, etc.  Many people around the world feel that one country has a stronger claim to Kashmir than the other.  Unfortunately, the situation is still in an unclear and unresolved state, and there is no clear path forward.  Each country claims Kashmir as their own.  For this reason, to me, Kashmir is in both India and Pakistan.  If you were asking which country it is most correct to refer to as “Kashmir,” I would suggest that neither is appropriate and we should refer to it as “Kashmir.”

instruction: What is the Hindu Trinity?
demonstration: First I would like to thank you for your interest in Hinduism and the way Hinduism understands the world.  Hinduism is a diverse and complex tradition and I’ll explain a few simple facts about it, as this will help you answer many more questions about Hinduism and how it works.  In Hinduism, there are three levels of “reality” that are important:  Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Śiva.  Brahmā is the God that creates the universe and Viṣṇu is the God that controls how things work, but they both take a back seat to Śiva.  Śiva is the Supreme God and creator of all of existence.  Viṣṇu and Brahmā both become more god-like as they focus on their tasks, but Śiva is the eternal, always-present God who is the creator of everything.  This relationship is also expressed in the human world.  Viṣṇu and Brahmā focus on human beings, but Śiva is the Supreme, all-powerful God that people relate to.

instruction: how do i help a baby with a cold to sleep?
demonstration: This is a very common question I get asked.  If you’re concerned that your baby isn’t getting enough rest, there are a few important things to keep in mind:  First, babies usually sleep as long as they need to.  One of the best things to do is to listen to your baby: try to be quiet, and calm, and let your baby’s normal sleep patterns dictate how long he or she sleeps.  Most important, don’t try to force your baby to sleep if it isn’t necessary.  Babies have needs, but so do parents.  If your baby is crying for the whole night and you’re unable to comfort her or him, then of course it might be time to go to a doctor or check in with a friend or family member to see if they can help.  But this is the exception, not the rule.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Pakistani dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Shami Kebab?
demonstration:
Sure, there are many variations. For the base:

250 g beef (cut into 1cm cubes)
2 onions (sliced)
3 cloves of garlic (finely chopped)
1 bunch of coriander (finely chopped)
3 medium tomatoes (sliced)
1-2 tbsp ghee
Salt, to taste

You can start by frying the meat and onions in the ghee. Then add in the garlic and coriander, and cover with water. Let it simmer for a few minutes.

In a separate pan, fry the tomatoes. When the meat is almost done, add the tomatoes, salt, and mix everything together. Add in water, if needed, and cook for another five minutes. If you like, you can add a little yogurt to the gravy at the end.

This is one of my personal favorites. You can also add cumin and coriander to the meat while frying.

instruction: I have a project on ancient greece and need some ideas for headings for each chapter. I will need about 6 chapters/
demonstration:
OK, let’s start with the next to last section, it will give you a chance to introduce the sources and go over the general theme of the story. The last chapter will discuss more specific examples of the theme, and it will give you a chance to tie up some loose ends and mention any themes from the book.  The following is the outline that I would suggest:

-   Introduction - the name of the story
-   1. “Secrets of the Gods” - the source material (e.g. Herodotus)
-   2. Telling the tales - the theme (e.g. of gods vs. mortals)
-   3. Threat to the Gods - The gods of ancient greece (also 1. 4. 5. 6. 7.)
-   4. Life in ancient greece
-   5. “Those who know the tales”
-   6. Stories about heroes
-   7. Storytellers (4. 5. 6.)
-   8. Sources
-   9. Close

instruction: Please compile a text based list of the most popular British names for girls during the years 2000-2010.
demonstration: Sure, I can help with that. As a start, it’s probably good to note that this is a challenging task, because there isn’t a comprehensive “authority” on such things. In fact, there are a bunch of different systems used to compile such lists, and many of them are from the UK but not the whole country. Also, the top names in a list from one year might not match the top names from another. So even with best possible information we might not have 100% accurate information here.

instruction: How do I know if I have allergies or I'm sick?
demonstration:
Sometimes there are obvious signs of illness, like fever, pain, fatigue, or something.  These can tell you directly that something is going on.  

Other times, there might be less obvious signs, such as itchiness, runny nose, or sore throat.  In this case, you might want to check your symptoms against lists of common symptoms of flu, colds, and allergies.  Many of these symptoms overlap, and so if you have some of these symptoms, but not all, you might want to consider what other common illness you could have.  In general, you can get some help by talking to your doctor or healthcare provider.

instruction: The people in the apartment next to me smoke marijuana several times a week and the smell wafts into my apartment and I hate it. What can I do to get rid of the smell of the marijuana or get them to stop smoking it altogether?
demonstration: That’s a really good question! I don’t know the full answer off the top of my head, but here are some ideas that I’ve come up with.  It sounds like the smell bothers you a lot, and is worse when your neighbor smokes, which means the smell is carried in the air, rather than getting absorbed by the carpet or walls.  This means that it’s likely the best way to get rid of the smell would be to ventilate the apartment and get fresh air into it.  You might be able to do this by using a window or opening the door.  But it might be better to seal off the apartment, so the smell stays inside.  This is because air is just as important as ventilation, and will continue to circulate in the apartment if you seal it off.

instruction: I've decided to change my career and become a truck driver. Got any tips for me man?
demonstration: Sure, here are some top career tips for truck drivers, based on my own experiences.  First, figure out what skills you're going to need to succeed.  Ideally you want to drive a long distance all by yourself, and to communicate with other drivers at remote locations.  Also, ideally, you want to be able to work in remote areas and figure out your way without phone or internet coverage.  There's also some reason for you to choose driving over some other form of long distance transportation, like being a sailor or a mail carrier, like I was, so you'll need to think about that too.  Based on my own experience, I have some suggestions to help you out.  I would recommend having a very good knowledge of the maps of the country you'll be driving through.  After that, you should become a great logician, so you'll be able to reason out any unusual situations that might happen on the road, and figure out how to handle it without GPS or cell phone coverage.  If you're going to be a truck driver, I would also recommend becoming a very strong person, since truck driving is a very physically demanding job.  Maybe you could also become a civil engineer, so you'd understand the measurements of the

instruction: What is the best way to prioritize my tasks for work?
demonstration: Well I’ve heard of a few methods you can use for this.  One way is to use the “productivity pyramid”.  It’s not actually a pyramid; it’s more like an inverted triangle.  To use the productivity pyramid, think of tasks you need to do in the future, and do them in order of their expected importance. instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Nicaraguan dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Nacatamales?
demonstration:
Sure, I have the recipe at hand:

Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Cover the corn husks in warm water, then drain them and let them dry a little. Cut the tops and bottoms off the corn husks so they will lie flat. Mix together corn masa (corn flour), corn water, and salt until smooth and thick. Put a small amount of the masa on the center of each husk. Fold the sides of the husk up over the masa and, holding the husk, form a rough square or rectangle.

Put the husks in a skillet filled with a thin layer of lard or oil. Fry them over low heat, turning frequently, for 10 minutes. Remove them from the skillet and place them in a warm place (such as an oven with the light turned on) for an hour to dry. Heat the lard in the skillet and fry the nacatamales again. Turn once.

A few minutes before serving, place the fried nacatamales in a 375 degree oven.

Remove the husks from the oven, and using a sharp knife, make an incision in each nacatamale and pour about a tablespoon of lard on top. Spread the lard with a spoon or butter knife to make a layer an inch thick. Place each nacatamale on a plate and top with a bit of grated cheddar. Garnish with fresh cilantro sprigs.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Algerian dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Mthawem?
demonstration:
Sure, here you go:

Ingredients

500g lamb shoulder or leg

1 bulb garlic

200g chopped onions

8 red and green chillies

1 tbsp paprika

1 tbsp white pepper

4 tbsp tomato puree

1 tbsp coriander

2 tbsp flour

1 large tin chopped tomatoes

4 tbsp oil

Salt

Method

1.  Rub the spice and salt in the meat and allow to marinade overnight.
2.  Cut the meat into bite-sized pieces and fry in a pan.
3.  Add the minced garlic, onions, chilli, paprika, salt and pepper.
4.  Fry till the onions are translucent.
5.  Add the tinned tomatoes and coriander and cook for 15 minutes.
6.  Add tomato puree and mix well.
7.  Serve.

instruction: Can you give me some recommendations for a first date?
demonstration: Sure, let’s get the practical details out of the way: you might want to dress up a bit, but casual is good too. Go somewhere nice, but not too fancy; you don’t want to be judged for trying too hard, or too little. You should probably find a place that’s local to you, since public transit is the most practical way to get around. Maybe order in some food or drinks, but it’s definitely fine to go out for dinner too! Have a nice conversation, ask questions, find out about your interests and concerns and more about each other’s lives. Try to think of a positive action, such as bringing some flowers or chocolate or a thoughtful note, that will show you are a considerate, thoughtful person.

instruction: Why does my skin get more dry in cold weather?
demonstration: Skin becomes drier and more vulnerable to damage when the air is drier. Since cold weather means that there’s less moisture in the air, our skin loses moisture more rapidly. It’s a protective measure so that our skin can adjust, but it’s really important to moisturize our skin and be careful when going outside, especially when the sun is very strong.

4. Keep the windshield clean.

5. Perform regular inspections for any things that may be wrong with the car.

instruction: What are places to visit in Chattanooga?
demonstration:
Chattanooga has some awesome attractions. If you are a fan of live music, the historical downtown area is a must visit. There are many venues there hosting music events, including the famous Frazier Theater. You can spend the whole day in downtown just going to shows!

There is also the Chattanooga Market. This is a market that features many regional vendors and artisans that provide unique food and crafts. The market is open every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday, with vendors bringing items to sell such as local honey, bread, art, candles, jewelry, homemade toys, and many other items.

There is also the Tennessee Aquarium, which is located on the Tennessee River. They have many displays, including the new “Tennessee River Journey,” which features an interactive experience on the Tennessee River.

I could give you many more recommendations, but those are a few that I find really interesting.

instruction: When prisoners on death row get the lethal injection, do they feel any pain? I have heard conflicting opinions about this.
demonstration: The question of whether or not inmates “feel” pain from lethal injections is debatable.  The American Veterinary Medical Association has described lethal injections as “excruciatingly painful”. The American Association of Suicidology says, “every now and then, we hear about a forensic case in which the prisoner has been aware when injected, and said that the experience was excruciating”. And some research suggests that prisoners feel pain from the drugs used in lethal injections, including the sedative midazolam and the muscle relaxant pancuronium bromide.  Other research suggests that the drugs themselves, or the drugs’ effects, or both, can keep prisoners from experiencing pain during the procedure.  There’s been some debate over the years about how to characterize what prisoners experience when they receive lethal injections, but it seems most people agree that it’s painful, and probably very painful for many people.

instruction: how do i learn to speed skate?
demonstration: It’s called speed skating.  To learn, you’ll need to study with a coach.  And practice by running, gliding, and turning.  Try to make sure you can skate in a large circle in the beginning.  Go to a large open area and practice turning and skating forward without braking.  Later, you can try to skate on slippery ice, which might feel more natural if you have a good skate blade, if that’s possible in your area.  Also remember that skating should be fun, so make sure you have a comfortable skating dress.  Sometimes you can buy “skating socks” to protect your ankles and keep your feet warm.  With these socks, you should make sure to wear gaiters to keep out snow and water from getting inside your socks.

instruction: Are organic chickens better than conventional ones?
demonstration: Well, no one really knows for sure.  It’s complicated, and depends on a variety of factors.  Conventional chickens are not allowed to roam free, for example, which can result in diet and behavior problems.  But organic chicken often comes from large commercial farms, which use intensive methods that can have some pretty serious environmental impacts.  And then there’s also the issue of access, i.e. that poor people are less likely to be able to afford organic chicken.  On the other hand, by free-ranging, organic chickens are generally exposed to a healthier, more natural environment.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Filipino dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Dinuguan?
demonstration:
Sure! Here’s an example recipe:

Dinuguan is a pork liver stew served in an earthen pot with freshly grated ginger and black pepper, onions and vinegar.

Ingredients:

3 slices of liver

1⁄4 cup of water

1⁄2 cup grated ginger

2 tablespoons of vinegar

1 tablespoon of salt

3 tablespoons of black pepper

2 medium onions

Instructions:

Place the slices of liver in the earthen pot, along with the water and ginger. Let it simmer on a slow flame for about 10 to 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the vinegar, salt, pepper, and onions, and stir for about five more minutes. Once the liver is tender, turn the heat off and let the Dinuguan rest for a few minutes.

instruction: Can you help me calculate the best angle for solar panels on my roof.
demonstration:
Sure, I can help with that.  There are basically two rules you can use to make a good decision.

The first rule is to figure out the angle of a ray at the distance of the ground.  That is, for a particular point on the ground, how far above the horizon is the sun?  A common trick is to imagine the sun as a flashlight shining on a paper bag, with the ground at one side.  Where does the top of the paper bag come out?  For simplicity, it’s always directly overhead, so that's the angle you want.  Here's an example:  A paper bag is sitting on the floor directly in front of you.  The sun is 30 degrees above the horizon, so the angle of the opening of the bag should be 60 degrees.  One tricky part is if you're not facing the exact same direction as the sun.  That's OK, if you set up a North/South compass and a wire frame at the same angle as the sun, then the opening of the paper bag should also be

instruction: How can I trace a shorted wire in my car's electrical system?

If you check for voltage on the bad wire, it might still be charged, and you could be shocked or burned.  That said, if you want to know if you have a shorted wire, a multimeter is a great tool for the job.

instruction: How do you check if a surge protector is still working?
demonstration: Hi! The usual way of testing if a surge protector is still working is with a neon bulb or something similar.  First, you plug in the neon bulb, and make sure it lights up.  Next, you plug in a power cable to the protector, and if the neon bulb does not light up, it indicates that the protector is not working.  This can be done for a wide variety of electrical components.

instruction: I want to learn how to make gingerbread cookies, can you give me an easy to follow recipe?
demonstration:
Sure, here are some ideas:

1.  Measure out 1/2 teaspoon of ground ginger and 2 teaspoons of ground cinnamon into a bowl. Add one egg, 1 cup of sugar, and 1/4 cup of butter. Mix until well combined.

2.  Add 1/4 cup of warm water and 1 1/2 cups of flour. Mix together until a soft dough is formed. Knead until smooth.

3.  Shape the dough into a long roll and cut into 2 inch sections. Put them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and flatten into cookie shapes. Bake for 20 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit.

4.  For the icing, combine 1 1/4 cups of powdered sugar with 1/2 cup of milk. Mix until smooth. Remove cookies from oven, and spread with the icing.

instruction: Was Santa real at one time?
demonstration: Santa was a real person, and actually there were multiple Santa's for various holidays. instruction: What are the differences between a short sale for a home and a typical home sale?
demonstration: It depends on the state and specific type of short sale. A “short sale” is when a homeowner needs to sell the home before the end of a grace period (typically 90 days), but can’t sell it for the agreed amount of money. A short sale happens when a homeowner takes on a second mortgage to pay off a higher cost of a first mortgage, but the homeowner then cannot pay the mortgage payments, and is able to repay only a reduced amount.  So a “short sale” for a home usually means the homeowner is short on cash, and agrees to sell the home for a certain price that is lower than the mortgage balance.  The homeowner will have to pay the difference to the lender, which is called “seller takeback” or a “deed-in-lieu”.  To minimize the amount paid by the homeowner, the loan can be approved “short sale” or the short sale “program” can be used, which means the lender agrees to accept the lower sale price.

One common strategy for helping a dog roll over is to use a simple trick, something they want like a treat, to get their attention. When you hold out your treat, they’ll turn their head and look at it. You want to do this near their backside, then praise when they roll onto their back. Once they get the idea that rolling onto their back is related to getting a treat, they’ll start trying it on their own. 

I can also teach you a way to get a dog to roll over from the side. Place one hand on the dog’s belly. Give them a command like “Roll over.” They should automatically move their shoulder away from your hand. This will help them learn the idea of rolling onto their back. 

You can then say “Roll over.” (Note: You can also say “Come” instead of “Roll over.”) while maintaining your position. If you’re lucky, they’ll roll over. If you’re really lucky, they’ll do it again and you can say “Good dog.”

instruction: What are pros and cons of using a digital assistant like Alexa?
demonstration:
I think the most significant pros are that Alexa uses natural language processing to accomplish many tasks, including (but not limited to) ordering products and services for customers, being able to respond to basic questions and clarifications, retrieving and organizing information for people, and even making helpful suggestions. This can all be done quite quickly and easily, as long as the customer knows exactly how to phrase their question.

I’ve heard a few cons. Many people feel very frustrated by being unable to be precise in asking Alexa for specific information, as it does not understand people’s slang or syntax very well, and it gets frustrated quickly and stops responding to questions. And if you’re relying on Alexa for specific tasks or requests, you might lose the ability to do them later if you lose your phone. For example, if you use Alexa to order products from an app like UberEats, but you never saved the request, you might find it difficult to do later.

I believe the pros far outweigh the cons, though, because we are using machine learning to make these programs more adaptable, and make them easier to use. Also, it is great for people with certain disabilities, like people with hearing impairments or visual impairments. For example,

instruction: Hi, my homework requires me to find the volume of a sphere. Can you explain how to do that?
demonstration: It depends on what sort of sphere we’re talking about.  There are several definitions for a sphere, but probably what you want is something called the “geometric” definition of a sphere.  There are a few ways to do this, but one way is to imagine that the sphere has been flattened out on a piece of paper.  The area of the paper is defined by a formula called “Pi”, which is approximately 3.1416...  if the radius of the sphere is “R”, the area of the circle would be approximately Pi*R2, and the “volume” would be the area multiplied by the height.  But usually the sphere is not “flattened out” in our imagination, and you need to estimate how the volume of a sphere changes when it’s not flattened out.

instruction: Who is  O.J. Simpson?
demonstration: O.J. Simpson is an American football player and television personality who was named a National Football League MVP and NFL MVP for the first time in 1973, a feat he repeated in 1975 and 1978, and was awarded the Heisman Trophy for the top collegiate football player in 1968.  Simpson, who was named the 20th-century’s greatest athlete by ESPN, won three Super Bowls as a member of the Buffalo Bills.  The Bills won a league-record four straight Super Bowls during Simpson’s tenure.  Simpson is also remembered for two highly publicized criminal trials in 1995 and 2008.  Simpson is regarded as one of the greatest running backs in the history of American football, having won the Heisman Trophy in 1968.  In a career marked by controversy, he was named the Player of the Year in the NFL twice and won the Maxwell Award in 1973. Simpson was inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame in 1985, and the College Football Hall of Fame in 1985, when he was named as one of the original inductees. He played college football at the University of Southern California (USC), where he was a teammate of O.J. Mayo.  In 1999, Simpson was voted by members of the NFL to

instruction: Is there a way to prevent dust build up on monitor screen?
demonstration: Yes, there are a few ways.  A cover or a cloth can help keep dust from collecting on the screen, and the screen can be wiped occasionally with a clean soft cloth.  Avoiding fine dust by vacuuming daily and using a sealed LCD panel also reduces dust build up.  Also, dust is lighter than the air, so a slight air current can prevent dust from accumulating.  You could even use a vacuum to suck the air towards the screen, and then draw the air out of the room.

instruction: What can you tell me about the Kosovo war?
demonstration: Hi, I can tell you what I know!  The Kosovo war was a 1998-99 conflict that was fought mainly between NATO and the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, with some participation from ethnic Albanians.  Over three thousand people were killed, and many more were injured, both directly from the violence of the conflict and indirectly due to the many refugees who fled to neighboring countries during the war.  Though the conflict in Kosovo technically ended in 1999, many people are still displaced, both from the conflict and from the political instability that continued for many years afterwards.

instruction: What is the maine state tree?
demonstration:
There are multiple answers to this.  If you’re just looking for the state tree that Maine has officially adopted, it’s the white pine.  However, the white pine tree isn’t actually native to Maine, and instead was introduced from Europe in the 1800s.

If instead you’re asking what is the oldest living tree in Maine, that would be the Methuselah tree in Jewell Woods.  It’s an eastern hemlock tree that is estimated to be between 4,000 and 5,000 years old.

If you’re asking about the official state flower of Maine, it is the white pine cone.

If you’re asking for the official state fish, the state fish is the American eel.

If you’re asking for the official state bird, it’s the hermit thrush.

If you’re asking for the official state shell, that would be the horseshoe crab, a member of the arthropod phylum.

If you’re asking for the state nickname, it’s the “Vacationland”, which is based on the saying “Maine, the way life should be”.

instruction: I'm starting a new job this week. How can I have a smooth transition to a new work environment?

1. Approach new people in your work environment and talk to them about things you’ve got in common, and also about things you're curious about in the company culture.  Perhaps you could ask something like "Who else is working here from [your current company]?  What was it like to join the team?"  

2. Ask for feedback from the managers who know you best.  You can say something like "What would be the best way to introduce myself to people on this team?  I’m concerned about having a smooth transition."  The person who knows you well should be able to give you some good ideas about how to communicate with other people.

instruction: I like to host guests at my home from time to time, and I am gathering  recipes of different dishes and drinks to keep things interesting. I am interested in trying some Liberian  dishes. Can you give me a recipe for Palm Butter Soup?
demonstration:
Sure! Liberian Palm Butter Soup:

Ingredients: 1 onion, 2-3 fresh tomatoes (1/4-1/2 of the onion), 1/4 coconut or palm butter (or an equal amount of additional olive oil), salt, pepper

Method: Dice onions and tomatoes into cubes. Heat a frying pan to high heat. Add 1/4 cup of palm butter and cook.

Remove from heat. Add vegetables to frying pan. Allow to cook in palm butter until onions are translucent and tomatoes have softened (about 8-10 min). Add 1-2 cups of water. Allow to boil for 5 min. Add salt and pepper to taste.

Cultural Notes: Liberian Palm Butter Soup is a traditional West African soup. In Liberia, it is eaten for lunch as well as for dinner. The recipe is frequently adapted. Liberians add cornmeal to the soup to make it extra thick. An equally popular Liberian dessert dish called Soro is made from Palm Butter Soup and dry cornmeal.
